name: Law Offices of Robert S. Gitmeid & Associates, PLLC
description_short: NYC-based bankruptcy and consumer defense law firm led by Robert S. Gitmeid, specializing in bankruptcy filing, debt negotiation, and fair debt collection practices.
description_long:
The Law Offices of Robert S. Gitmeid & Associates, PLLC is a Manhattan-based consumer advocacy law firm established with 16 years of practice experience. The firm is led by Managing Member and General Counsel Robert S. Gitmeid, who is admitted to practice in New York State, New Jersey, Florida, and the U.S. Federal Courts of the Southern and Eastern Districts of New York. The firm positions itself as a consumer-focused practice built on earned trust rather than institutional legacy.

The firm offers three primary service areas: bankruptcy filing and consultation (Chapter 7 and Chapter 13), debt negotiation and settlement as alternatives to bankruptcy, and fair debt collection defense under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA). They position themselves as educators who help clients understand both the benefits and drawbacks of bankruptcy relative to other debt relief options. Their approach emphasizes informed decision-making and pragmatic solutions tailored to individual circumstances.

The firm distinguishes itself through its focus on consumer advocacy against creditors and debt collectors, using what they describe as "innovative legal theories and strategies at the forefront of consumer law." They specifically address FDCPA violations and abusive debt collection tactics, and market themselves as representing consumers against "large and aggressive creditors." The firm's stated philosophy emphasizes compassion alongside results-oriented representation.

As a law firm rather than a financial services provider, this company serves a different function than debt relief or credit repair services—they provide legal representation and counsel. Potential clients should understand this is attorney-led legal services, not debt settlement negotiation or credit repair. The firm's specialization in bankruptcy and consumer defense suggests they primarily work with individuals facing serious debt challenges rather than those seeking preventative credit management.
address: 180 Maiden Ln 27th Floor, New York, NY 10038

company_info.headquarters: 180 Maiden Ln 27th Floor, New York, NY 10038

pros: Licensed attorney (Robert S. Gitmeid) admitted to practice in multiple states (NY, NJ, FL) and federal courts
Explicit focus on Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA) violations and defense against abusive collectors
Offers debt negotiation as an alternative to bankruptcy, providing clients with multiple options
16 years of established practice history in New York City consumer law
Provides bankruptcy education to help clients make informed decisions between Chapter 7, Chapter 13, and alternatives
Addresses emotional stress and privacy invasion tactics used by debt collectors
Consumer advocacy focus explicitly stated as core mission
cons: Limited geographic availability—primary office at single Manhattan location despite multi-state bar admissions
No information provided on fees, payment plans, or cost transparency for bankruptcy or legal services
Website content is incomplete (cuts off mid-sentence in FDCPA section), suggesting dated or poorly maintained web presence
No client testimonials, case results, or specific outcomes disclosed
No mention of free consultations or initial assessment options
best_for: New York and New Jersey residents facing creditor lawsuits or aggressive debt collection practices
Consumers deciding between bankruptcy filing and debt negotiation alternatives
Individuals experiencing harassment or violations under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act
diagnosis: This firm is best for consumers in the NYC/NJ area facing serious debt challenges—bankruptcy filing, creditor lawsuits, or aggressive collection harassment—who need legal representation rather than debt settlement services. The main caveat is that they are a legal services provider, not a debt relief or credit repair company, and bankruptcy should only be considered after consultation with qualified counsel regarding all available alternatives.
services: Debt negotiation and settlement negotiations with creditors
Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA) defense and litigation
Bankruptcy education and informed decision consultation
Creditor challenge and consumer advocacy representation
Federal court representation (Southern District and Eastern District of New York)
Multi-state legal representation (New York, New Jersey, Florida)
